# Contentkit Documentation

A robots.txt-style index of the Contentkit documentation for LLMs. Contentkit is
an API-first headless Markdown mini-CMS with semantic visual composition and
declarative information patterns. It turns immutable revisions into multilingual
websites, documentation, reports and infographics.
Visual content follows Semantic AST → Narrative → Composition → deterministic
HTML/SVG/PNG, using a declarative repository-owned Pattern Registry. Each entry
below has a title, path and one-line summary. For the complete
documentation inlined into a single file, see [docs/llms-full.txt](docs/llms-full.txt).

Normal pages and posts may embed selected semantic directives as accessible
responsive HTML. Their Semantic AST reports `presentation: embedded`; the
document remains editorial prose and does not gain standalone SVG/PNG exports.
Use `layout: composition` when the complete document must resolve through a
Pattern Package and export as an independent visual.

> Local API: http://127.0.0.1:4050 — OpenAPI 3.1 spec at `/openapi.json`.
> Production API: your own deployment, e.g.
> https://contentkit-api.example.com. Management endpoints require
> `Authorization: Bearer <key>`. Public contact/comment endpoints are protected
> by Turnstile, honeypot fields and rate limits.

## Start here

- [README](README.md): What Contentkit is, local zero-config setup, content upload, preview/release flow and verification commands.
- [Example page](examples/page.de.md): German Markdown page with supported frontmatter.
- [Example post](examples/post.de.md): German Markdown blog post with supported frontmatter.
- [Template examples](docs/TEMPLATES.md): Controlled site presets, layouts,
  versioned routes, hierarchy fields and real English Markdown documents.
- [Visual compositions](docs/VISUAL_COMPOSITIONS.md): beginner-friendly
  architecture, semantic Markdown, 81 declarative patterns, AI-agent workflow,
  headless planning/compile APIs, themes, gallery and migration.
- [Information blocks plan](docs/INFORMATION_BLOCKS.md): researched mapping for
  dashboards, application shells, pricing, galleries, stats, FAQ, code examples
  and data tables, including phased implementation and verification.
- [Examples](examples/README.md): ownership and regeneration rules for authored
  inputs, the visual gallery and the registry snapshot.
- [Reports and dashboards](docs/REPORTS.md): `layout: composition` with
  `composition.format: report`, controlled dashboard primitives, table-driven
  charts, cadence catalogs, primary-metric projection on product home pages,
  tokens and limits.
- [Product analytics API](docs/PRODUCT_ANALYTICS.md): Bounded site-scoped
  release, content, reader, webhook, audio and engagement aggregates for
  external collectors using the existing `content:read` scope.
- [Example report](examples/reports/quarterly.en.md): Complete quarterly report
  with metrics, cards, progress and all four chart types.
- [Reader access control](docs/ACCESS_CONTROL.md): Personal reader accounts,
  groups, release-scoped exact/prefix policies, sessions and protected discovery.
- [Benchmarks](docs/BENCHMARKS.md): Reproducible 1,000-document corpus, results
  and CI regression budgets.
- [OpenAPI snapshot](docs/openapi.json): Committed canonical OpenAPI 3.1 snapshot used by the docs drift check.

## API & usage

- Endpoints: `GET /` service descriptor on the API host, `GET /health`,
  `GET /ready`, `GET /metrics`, `GET /openapi.json`, `GET /llms.txt`,
  `GET /llms-full.txt`, plus `/v1/*` management routes and public submission
  routes.
- Pattern discovery: `GET /v1/composition-patterns` and
  `GET /v1/composition-patterns/{pattern}`. Site-scoped agents use
  `/v1/sites/{site}/compositions/recommend`, `/validate` and `/compile`, then
  fetch published `.svg` or `.png` representations with `content:read`.
- Story selection: `GET /v1/publishing-guides` and
  `GET /v1/publishing-guides/{guide}` describe the question, story arc,
  required evidence, rejection conditions, compatible patterns, and examples
  for reports, diagrams, and code explanations.
- Instance semantics: chart directives may add `question`, `insight`, `action`
  and `limitation`; Mermaid fences may add the same quoted metadata plus
  `title`. The Semantic AST carries these narrative fields and links technical
  diagrams to their publishing guide.
- Reader endpoints on a published site host: `GET|POST /_contentkit/login`,
  `POST /_contentkit/logout`, `GET /_contentkit/session`,
  `GET /_contentkit/navigation.json` and
  `GET /_contentkit/search-index.json`.
- Reader administration: `/v1/sites/{site}/access/users`,
  `/v1/sites/{site}/access/groups` and `/v1/sites/{site}/access/rules`, plus
  membership and session-revocation endpoints, all with `site:admin`.
- Management flow: create site → upload Markdown + assets → build preview →
  publish release → optionally activate an older release for rollback.
- Markdown upload accepts raw `text/markdown` or `multipart/form-data` with a
  `document` field and optional `asset:<path>` file fields.
- API keys are scoped. Use `site:admin` for site/key administration,
  `content:read content:write release:write` for publishing, and
  `moderation:write` for comments/contact moderation. Send the key as
  `Authorization: Bearer ck_...` or `X-API-Key: ck_...`; the raw key is shown
  only once by `POST /v1/api-keys`.
- Auth failures are distinct: `401 {"error":"unauthorized"}` means the key was
  not accepted (missing/revoked/expired or hash mismatch — often a wrong
  `CONTENTKIT_KEY_PEPPER`), while `403 {"error":"insufficient_scope", ...}` means
  a valid key that lacks the required scope or site grant. See llms-full section 3.
- Webhooks are signed with Standard Webhooks headers
  (`webhook-id`/`-timestamp`/`-signature: v1,<b64 HMAC-SHA256>`); the `whsec_`
  secret is shown once and is used verbatim (prefix included) as the HMAC key.
  See llms-full section 13.
- OpenAPI 3.1 is generated by the service and should be treated as the
  authoritative HTTP contract.

## Complete feature map

- Site administration: create/read/update sites, locales, domains and design/content
  settings such as hero text, accent color, socials, profile image and Turnstile
  site key.
- Controlled presentation: `portfolio`, `product-docs`, `wiki`,
  `knowledge-base`, `product` and `changelog` site presets; `standard`, `docs`,
  `wiki`, `knowledge`, `landing`, `changelog`, `composition` and legacy `report`
  page layouts; hierarchical
  navigation and current/archived documentation versions.
- Visual compositions: 81 declarative Markdown+YAML Pattern Packages,
  deterministic recommendation/validation, formal Layout and Render Trees,
  container-aware structural fallbacks, neutral light/dark themes, standalone
  SVG/PNG, typed units and time, content budgets, spec examples and
  AI-readable diagnostics. Every pattern includes a narrative contract. The
  review gallery uses the validated SVG baseline and its PNG export; semantic
  HTML remains available but is not advertised as pixel-equivalent.
- Visual reports: neutral groups, cards, metrics, badges and progress;
  `bar`, `line`, `area` and `donut` charts take one Markdown table and become
  accessible light/dark SVG assets at build time. Source tables remain visible;
  no client chart JavaScript or executable chart configuration is published.
  Optional typed data shapes cover ranges, changes, divergence, Likert,
  correlation, distributions, matrices, waterfalls, hierarchies, flows,
  uncertainty, calendars, coordinate points, equal-area region tiles and
  samples; agents match them through `accepts.data_shapes` in the registry.
  Two or more level-two headings become a responsive local report navigation.
- Reader access: personal usernames and scrypt passwords, named groups,
  exact/prefix path policies and release-scoped authorization. Protected pages
  and media require an HttpOnly reader session and are omitted from public
  navigation, search, sitemap, feeds and LLM files. Protected static pages can
  render only exact same-grant navigation/home cards; product report sites link
  a cadence-aware current-report catalog plus history from exact same-grant
  reports.
- Content authoring: immutable revisions for `page`, `post` and `project`
  Markdown documents, scheduled revisions, tags, translations and asset uploads.
- Static generation: home, blog, project, archive, tag index, tag, search,
  contact, content pages, RSS, per-tag feeds, sitemap, robots, 404, canonical
  links, hreflang, OpenGraph, Twitter cards and JSON-LD (BlogPosting,
  BreadcrumbList, FAQPage).
- AI readability (GEO): per-locale `llms.txt`/`llms-full.txt`, a raw Markdown
  twin per post and report (`.../index.md`), an AI share row on posts ("copy as Markdown", "open in
  Claude/ChatGPT" — the reader's own assistant, no site→AI data flow) and
  authored `tldr`/`faq` frontmatter rendered on the page and in JSON-LD.
- Publishing: expiring previews, atomic releases, scheduled publishing through
  `/v1/publish-due`, and pointer rollback to an existing release.
- Public interaction: contact form, post comments, Turnstile, honeypot, rate
  limit, moderation queue and signed webhook notifications.
- Content lifecycle webhooks: `contentkit.content.published`,
  `contentkit.content.unpublished` and `contentkit.release.published` events
  emitted transactionally with release activation. See llms-full section 13.
- Content modeling light: author-owned `extra:` custom fields and `related:`
  post references in frontmatter, validated on write and stored verbatim in
  the revision metadata; page rendering is a per-site opt-in via
  `settings.content.show_extra`. See llms-full section 6.
- Headless JSON read API: `GET /v1/sites/{site}/published` (filters, keyset
  pagination, weak ETag) and `GET /v1/sites/{site}/published/{kind}/{locale}/{slug}`
  (Markdown source verbatim plus on-demand rendered HTML, strong ETag/304)
  behind `content:read` scoped keys — delivery stays static. See llms-full
  section 4.
- Server-side full-text search: `GET /v1/sites/{site}/search?q=` ranks
  published content with locale-aware PostgreSQL stemming (de/en) and returns
  `<mark>`-highlighted headlines, behind `content:read` scoped keys — published
  sites keep their static client-side search. See llms-full section 4.
- Product analytics API: `GET /v1/sites/{site}/stats/{releases|content|readers|webhooks|audio|engagement}`
  returns privacy-safe dense UTC aggregates for bounded `from`/`to` windows,
  behind existing site-scoped `content:read` keys. ContentKit does not own the
  downstream cockpit or reporting store. See llms-full section 21.
- Theming: per-site design tokens via `settings.theme.tokens` (allowlisted
  stylesheet custom properties, scalar or `{ light, dark }` values,
  `chart_1` through `chart_5` color report SVGs, `settings.accent` stays the
  primary shorthand) plus a size-capped
  `settings.theme.custom_css` escape hatch — a theme is a token assignment,
  never a template override. See llms-full section 5.

## Content model and publishing

- [Architecture](docs/ARCHITECTURE.md): Content lifecycle, migration ownership,
  security boundaries and static output model.
- Pages, posts and projects are content items. Each write creates an immutable
  revision. Releases render selected revisions into a new immutable storage
  prefix and atomically move the active release pointer.
- A release overlays `revision_ids` on the published set and removes
  `retire_item_ids` from it. `DELETE /v1/content/{item}/published` unpublishes
  a single item; revisions stay immutable, so republishing brings it back.
- Static output includes locale-prefixed routes, a year-grouped archive,
  a tag index and tag pages, RSS and per-tag feeds, sitemap, robots, a per-site
  `llms.txt` and `llms-full.txt`, canonical metadata, OpenGraph and JSON-LD.
- This file describes Contentkit itself and is served only on the API host
  (`CONTENTKIT_PUBLIC_URL`). On a published site domain, `/llms.txt` is that
  site's own generated index, not this document.

## Operating & deployment

- [Deployment](docs/DEPLOYMENT.md): Single self-contained binary, production
  environment, systemd deployment, release pipeline and required secrets.
- [Webhooks](docs/WEBHOOKS.md): Outbound webhook events, Standard Webhooks
  signature verification and scheduled publishing.
- [Read-aloud audio](docs/audio.md): TTS narration lifecycle
  (publish → job → worker → asset → auto-rebuild), backfill, deletion, job
  monitoring, character budgets and the blogcast feed.
- [Security](SECURITY.md): Vulnerability reporting and security posture.

## Development

- `npm start`: local zero-config stack with Docker PostgreSQL and local
  Storage/Webhook boundary.
- `npm run demo:profile`: creates a personal profile page, blog post and
  legal-notice page and publishes them locally.
- `npm test`, `npm run test:integration`, `npm run test:e2e:local`: unit,
  integration and local binary end-to-end verification.
- `npm run test:contract`, `npm run test:smoke`: API/router contract tests and
  a real-document multi-template build.
- `npm run benchmark`: deterministic 1,000-document build plus a separately
  timed 50-report/200-chart build, access resolution and password verification.
- `npm run docs:gen-openapi` updates the committed OpenAPI snapshot; `npm run
  check:docs-drift` fails when OpenAPI and LLM docs drift apart.
